content.text:
Here are the results:

---

## (1) Complete list of files that use the `Token` struct from `src/token.rs`

1. `src/event/source.rs`
2. `src/io_source.rs`
3. `src/net/tcp/listener.rs`
4. `src/net/tcp/stream.rs`
5. `src/net/udp.rs`
6. `src/net/uds/datagram.rs`
7. `src/net/uds/listener.rs`
8. `src/net/uds/stream.rs`
9. `src/poll.rs`
10. `src/sys/shell/selector.rs`
11. `src/sys/shell/waker.rs`
12. `src/sys/unix/selector/epoll.rs`
13. `src/sys/unix/selector/kqueue.rs`
14. `src/sys/unix/selector/poll.rs`
15. `src/sys/unix/selector/stateless_io_source.rs`
16. `src/sys/unix/sourcefd.rs`
17. `src/sys/unix/waker/eventfd.rs`
18. `src/sys/unix/waker/kqueue.rs`
19. `src/sys/unix/waker/pipe.rs`
20. `src/sys/wasi/mod.rs`
21. `src/sys/windows/event.rs`
22. `src/sys/windows/named_pipe.rs`
23. `src/sys/windows/waker.rs`
24. `src/waker.rs`

## (2) Total count

**24 files** (excluding the definition file `src/token.rs` itself)

## (3) Tool calls made

| # | Tool | Arguments |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `Bash` | `command: "kdb refs src/token.rs -s Token"` |

That's it — a single `kdb refs` call was all that was needed.
